response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly capable Staff-level engineer with nearly two decades of experience, current TypeScript/Node.js work, React, AWS, Kubernetes, and even LLM/AI integration — a technical profile that exceeds every required and nice-to-have qualification listed. However, the role is explicitly entry-level with junior/mid expectations, and hiring a Staff engineer into it creates a near-certain mismatch: misaligned compensation expectations, rapid disengagement from low-complexity work, and high flight risk. The technical fit is strong, but the seniority delta is too large to overlook for a role scoped at this level.
strengths: Full TypeScript/Node.js stack proficiency with production-scale experience at Optilyz and re:cinq, covering exactly the languages and patterns the role requires
Covers all nice-to-have qualifications: LLM/AI agent infrastructure, AWS CDK, and Kubernetes cluster deployments via GISCollective
Demonstrated culture of software craftsmanship (TDD, pairing, clean architecture, trunk-based development) that aligns with the role's interest in clean software architecture
concerns: Severely overqualified — Staff/Senior with 17+ years of experience applying for an entry-level role signals either a major career pivot with unclear motivation or an expectation mismatch that will surface quickly
PostgreSQL is not specifically highlighted; primary database experience skews toward MongoDB and non-relational stores, with SQL listed generically in skills

Nest.js is not mentioned anywhere in the resume, and the candidate's current focus on AI-agent tooling and MCP frameworks may indicate interests diverging from the CRUD-heavy portal and CRM integration work this role entails
